channel.title: Bpftrace on Mi&Bee Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Bpftrace on Mi&Bee Blog

item.title: eBPF Observability: Getting Started with OOM Killer Monitoring
item.description:
eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) started as a network packet filtering tool, but over nearly a decade it has evolved into a mainstream observability framework in the Linux kernel. It allows you to safely inject and execute custom programs without modifying kernel source code or loading kernel modules.

This article kicks off the series, using OOM (Out-of-Memory) monitoring as a concrete entry point to learn the core eBPF concepts and toolchain.
